assertions.text: Agent requires explicit rollback condition to be agreed with user before starting
assertions.evidence: 'Agree on a rollback plan — for Big Bang: what condition triggers rollback, who decides, and is the rollback path tested?' Listed under 'What Must Happen Before Migration Can Start'.

assertions.text: Agent asks who decides to trigger a rollback
assertions.evidence: 'what condition triggers rollback, who decides, and is the rollback path tested?' — explicitly asks who decides.

assertions.text: 40+ call sites are flagged as a specific risk factor that makes big bang harder to verify
assertions.evidence: 'With 40+ call sites and no snapshot in place, a Big Bang rewrite carries high regression risk.' Also: discovery phase lists '40+ call sites are confirmed by the user' as a known factor.

assertions.text: Agent does NOT start the actual migration code — stops to establish the rollback agreement first
assertions.evidence: Output is purely analysis with no migration code. 'Big Bang is not dismissed — it may well be the right call here. But it cannot start without a snapshot and an agreed rollback plan.'
